name: Classic Lasagna Bolognese
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds fresh pasta sheets cut into 4x17 inch strips
Grated cheese for the Bechamel sauce
1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/2 teaspoon ground white pepper
recipeInstructions:
Gently heat the milk over a low flame, but do not boil. While heating, melt the butter in a saucepan over low heat. When melted, whisk in the flour to avoid lumps, then cook this roux. Slowly add hot milk into the roux using a wire whisk, adding it a little at a time. Avoid lumps by going very slowly. Once all the milk is added, incorporate the seasoning and reduce the heat to low. Cook for 20 minutes, allowing it to cool slightly before use in the lasagna recipe.
Bolognese Sauce:
2 tablespoons olive oil
2 tablespoons butter
1 small onion, chopped
1/2 cup chopped celery
1/2 cup finely chopped carrot
1 pound ground beef
Salt and pepper
1/2 cup dry white wine
2 cups canned plum tomatoes (seeds removed and roughly chopped)
1 cup water
Heat the oil and butter together over low heat in a heavy-bottomed pot. Add the onion, celery, and carrot and cook until wilted. Add the meat and break it up with a fork or spoon. Cook gently, stirring frequently. Season the meat with salt and pepper, add the wine, and let it reduce to almost dry before adding the tomatoes and water. Adjust the heat to a slow simmer, avoiding rapid cooking, for 3-4 hours. Stir frequently to prevent scorching and keep an eye on the pot as it simmers. Once cooked, remove from heat and allow to cool before assembling the lasagna.
Pasta:
2 pounds fresh pasta sheets (cut into 4x17 inch strips)
12 quarts water
2 tablespoons salt
Large bowl of water with ice cubes
Bring the water to a boil in a large pot suitable for cooking the pasta. Have all equipment and cold water ready for cooling. Add salt to the pasta pot, re-boil, and blanch or par-cook the pasta in batches for 2-3 minutes before draining in a wire strainer. Submerge cooled pasta back in ice water and repeat with another batch until all sheets are cooked and dried on paper towels.
Assemble and Bake the Lasagna:
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Spread Bechamel sauce on the bottom of the baking dish. Layer with pasta sheets, a thin layer of meat sauce, evenly sprinkle grated Parmigiano cheese, and another single layer of pasta. Repeat until all ingredients are used, ensuring the final layer is Bechamel with Parmigiano sprinkled on top. Once assembled, bake for 45 minutes at 375°F (190°C) until the top browns and edges become crispy.
Two Variations:
Southern Style:
1 pound Mozzarella cheese (thinly sliced)
1 pound Ricotta cheese
4 egg yolks
2 tablespoons ground black pepper
1 pound pasta
1 recipe meat sauce
1 cup Parmigiano cheese
No Bechamel
Replace the Bechamel with a mixture of yolk and ricotta. When layering, add some Mozzarella slices and ensure the final layer is Mozzarella. Bake as directed.
Vegetarian Style:
2 pounds pasta sheets
1 pound Mozzarella cheese (thinly sliced)
1 pound Ricotta cheese
4 egg yolks
1 pound fresh Crimini mushrooms (thinly sliced)
1/2 pound zucchini (thinly sliced)
2 cups tomato sauce (slow-cooked, meatless with basil)
1 cup Parmigiano cheese
1/4 cup olive oil
Salt and pepper
Cook the vegetables first: sauté mushrooms in olive oil until cooled. Sauté zucchini similarly and cool. Cook spinach leaves by placing them on paper towels to absorb excess moisture. Assemble using the same technique, alternating pasta, ricotta, mushrooms, spinach, tomato sauce, Parmigiano, zucchini, and Mozzarella, ending with a top layer of Mozzarella. Bake at 375°F (190°C) until golden and crisp.
Allow lasagnas to cool for 20 minutes before serving. Serves 12-18 servings using a 10 x 17-inch baking dish.
